RAD.Name: 1L13-3 Nebo-SV
RAD.Note: The 1L13-3 Nebo radar ECCM features include pulse to pulse frequency agility. It is designed to reject chaff automatically.

RAD.Name: 1S91
RAD.Type: Fire Control, Short range Target Acquisition

RAD.Name: 76N6
RAD.Type: Low alt search and Target Acquisition

RAD.Name: 9S86
RAD.Type: Range and radial velocity finding radar

RAD.Name: AN/MPQ-37
RAD.Note: Provide ranging data when the other radars are jammed by countermeasures

RAD.Name: AN/MPQ-50 PAR
RAD.Note: To detect moving targets and continually furnish target range and azimuth to plan position indicators (PPI).

RAD.Name: AN/MPQ-51
RAD.Note: Provide ranging data when the other radars are jammed by countermeasures.

RAD.Name: AN/MPQ-55
RAD.Note: Covers the low-altitude zone, the primary zone of consideration for Hawk.

RAD.Name: AN/MPQ-62
RAD.Note: Covers the low-altitude zone, the primary zone of consideration for Hawk.

RAD.Name: AN/MPQ-65
RAD.Type: Passive electronically scanned radar, Fire Control Radar

RAD.Name: Dagger Radar
RAD.IFF: Cossor Mark X or XII IFF
RAD.Note: A high elevation guard beam automatically switches off the transmissions when the presence of an anti-radiation missile is detected.

RAD.Name: Giraffe
RAD.UsedBy: RBS, BAMSE, Stinger, Rapier, Mistral AA guns

RAD.Name: SNR-75
RAD.Note: Scanners exhibit 'flapping' motion in operation, E version have 2 additional parabolic dishes
